description: Use the N/log module to manually access methods for logging script execution details. Log messages appear on the Execution Log subtab of the script deployment for deployed scripts, or on the Execution Log subtab of the SuiteScript Debugger if you are debugging a script.

methods.name: audit
methods.description: Logs an Audit message. Audit messages appear on the Execution Log subtab if the Log Level on the script deployment is set to Audit or Debug. Use this method for scripts in production.

params.name: options.title
params.description: The text to appear in the Title column on the Execution Log subtab. Maximum length is 99 characters.

params.name: options.details
params.description: You can pass any value. If the value is a JavaScript Object type, JSON.stringify(obj) is called on the object before displaying.

methods.name: debug
methods.description: Logs a Debug message. Suitable only for testing scripts. Do not use Debug level for active scripts in production.

params.name: options.title
params.description: The text to appear in the Title column on the Execution Log subtab.

params.name: options.details
params.description: You can pass any value. If the value is a JavaScript Object type, JSON.stringify(obj) is called on the object before displaying.

methods.name: emergency
methods.description: Logs an Emergency message. Use to show only the most critical errors in the script log.

params.name: options.title
params.description: The text to appear in the Title column on the Execution Log subtab.

methods.name: error
methods.description: Logs an Error message. Use to show only unexpected script errors.

params.name: options.title
params.description: The text to appear in the Title column on the Execution Log subtab.
